title: Teaching & Mentoring
text: I am committed to training the next generation of ecologists, with a focus on quantitative methods and reproducible science.

title: Guilherme Maricato
text:
[cite_start]I am an ecologist with over 10 years of experience in marine mammal conservation[cite: 8]. [cite_start]Currently, I am a Postdoctoral Research Fellow at the Federal University of Rio de Janeiro (UFRJ) and a Courtesy Postdoctoral Associate at Florida International University (FIU)[cite: 10].
[cite_start]My research focuses on Spatial Ecology and Species Distribution Modeling (SDM)[cite: 9], aiming to understand how marine megafauna navigate anthropogenic landscapes. [cite_start]I integrate field data, remote sensing, and statistical modeling to mitigate conflicts between biodiversity and human activities[cite: 10].
[cite_start]I am also a strong advocate for Open Science practices and reproducibility in ecology[cite: 32].



Spatial Ecology & SDM
Marine Conservation
Human-Wildlife Interactions
Open Science

[cite_start]My research focuses on Spatial Ecology and Species Distribution Modeling (SDM)[cite: 9], aiming to understand how marine megafauna navigate anthropogenic landscapes. text: I believe in making science accessible beyond academia. I frequently contribute to media outlets and educational projects.

title: Research
text: My research combines quantitative ecology with conservation biology to answer questions across three main themes:

title: Research
text:
1. Species Distribution Modeling & Spatial Ecology
I apply and develop spatial analysis frameworks to predict species occurrence and habitat suitability. [cite_start]My work investigates how environmental variables drive the distribution of cetaceans, from coastal dolphins (Sotalia guianensis, Tursiops truncatus) to oceanic whales[cite: 9, 14, 16].

Key Focus: Understanding the ecological niche of marine mammals in dynamic oceanographic environments.

title: Research
text:
2. Human Impacts & Marine Spatial Planning
A major part of my research assesses the overlap between marine biodiversity and anthropogenic stressors. [cite_start]I evaluate exposure to threats to identify conflict zones and propose management actions[cite: 10, 105].

[cite_start]Current Projects: Investigating marine protected areas (MPAs) effectiveness for Bryde’s whales and bottlenose dolphins[cite: 105].
[cite_start]Past Work: Assessment of aerial behaviors of humpback whales in Northeastern Brazil[cite: 18].

title: Research
text:
3. Marine Mammal Biology & Conservation
[cite_start]Since 2012, I have been monitoring marine mammal populations in Brazil[cite: 8]. This involves studying their behavior, social structure, and health parameters to understand population dynamics.

[cite_start]Field Experience: I have served as an associate researcher for the Maresis Project, Humpback Whale Project, and Whales & Dolphins of Rio Project[cite: 20, 21, 23].
